jokes.text: If wedding invitations were left up to men... we'd just drive around sticking fliers in windshields.
jokes.setup: Jerry opens with premise about male approach to wedding invitations
jokes.punchline: Reducing formal invitations to casual windshield fliers
jokes.explanation: Strong opening observational humor with perfect economy and relatable gender stereotype subversion

jokes.text: Not even typed up either. Just magic markers, xeroxed, you know: 'Party.'
jokes.setup: Building on the windshield flier concept
jokes.punchline: Reducing it further to handwritten 'Party' - ultimate male simplification
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation that pushes the male laziness concept to absurd extreme

jokes.text: What's needed is a divorce announcement. 'Mr. and Mrs. Fred Johnson request the honour of your presence... at the returning of their daughter back to Mr. and Mrs. Fred Johnson.'
jokes.setup: Jerry proposes divorce announcements as equally valid
jokes.punchline: Formal wedding invitation language applied to divorce with 'returning of their daughter'
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of wedding tradition with precise formal language parody

jokes.text: What's the difference? You just read it and mail it right back.
jokes.setup: Susan objects to ugly wedding invitations
jokes.punchline: George's complete dismissal of wedding invitation aesthetics
jokes.explanation: Perfect George - practical to the point of missing all social nuance

jokes.text: It takes a lot of moisture to make them stick. So we pick up some Elmer's.
jokes.setup: Store clerk explains glue problem with cheap invitations
jokes.punchline: George's solution is to just buy separate glue
jokes.explanation: George's penny-pinching logic taken to absurd lengths - foreshadows the toxic glue plot

jokes.text: No, Susan. No, no, it's Lilly. I think I know my own name. It's Susan. Well, you look like a Lilly.
jokes.setup: Kramer calls Susan by wrong name
jokes.punchline: Insists she looks like a Lilly even after correction
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer obliviousness - doubles down instead of apologizing

jokes.text: Can I do this? I can't do this. Look at me. Look at me, I can't do this. I can't do it. Help me, Jerry, help me.
jokes.setup: George realizes wedding is becoming real
jokes.punchline: Complete panic spiral with desperate plea to Jerry
jokes.explanation: George's neurosis in full display - captures his trapped feeling perfectly

jokes.text: I'd rather be unhappy for the rest of my life than to go through that.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggests breaking up with Susan
jokes.punchline: George prefers lifelong misery to one difficult conversation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant distillation of George's conflict avoidance - dark but hilarious truth

jokes.text: I disappear in a sea of people for, like, six months, a year.
jokes.setup: George considers writing breakup letter
jokes.punchline: Planning to flee to China and hide among population
jokes.explanation: George's cowardice taken to international scale - absurd but believable for him

jokes.text: It's easier to blend into a sea of people in Staten Island than China.
jokes.setup: Jerry points out job complications with China plan
jokes.punchline: George's illogical comparison of Staten Island to China for hiding
jokes.explanation: Absurd geography logic with subtle Staten Island dig

jokes.text: Freedom and no clothes is a lot better than no freedom with clothes.
jokes.setup: George worries about leaving clothes behind
jokes.punchline: Jerry's philosophical take on freedom vs possessions
jokes.explanation: Clever aphorism that sounds wise but is really about avoiding marriage

jokes.text: And what, hope for a crash? It happens. Do you know the odds on a crash? It's a million to one. It's something. It's hope.
jokes.setup: George wishes Susan would take a plane somewhere
jokes.punchline: George finding hope in million-to-one crash odds
jokes.explanation: Darkly hilarious desperation - George finding optimism in tragic statistics

jokes.text: You don't ask. You tell.
jokes.setup: George says he'll ask Susan about Elaine being usher
jokes.punchline: Elaine's aggressive approach to wedding party inclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Elaine assertiveness in four words

jokes.text: Start smoking. Does she hate cigarettes? Yes, she hates cigarettes. But you don't smoke. No.
jokes.setup: George asks how to end relationship
jokes.punchline: Elaine suggests taking up smoking specifically because Susan hates it
jokes.explanation: Clever reverse psychology approach - deliberately adopt dealbreaker habit

jokes.text: So it's just gonna be me and Kramer? Yeah, just you and Kramer. Me and Kramer.
jokes.setup: Jerry realizes friends are all getting married
jokes.punchline: Dawning horror of being left alone with just Kramer
jokes.explanation: Jerry's existential dread captured perfectly - the repetition sells the horror

jokes.text: A periscope in a car, so you can see traffic. How are you gonna drive while you're looking through a periscope? Besides which, it's not a submarine.
jokes.setup: Kramer presents car periscope invention
jokes.punchline: Jerry's logical dismantling of the absurd concept
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer invention logic met with Jerry's reality check

jokes.text: It's stupid! Stupid. It's stupid.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining why car periscope won't work
jokes.punchline: Jerry losing it and just repeating 'stupid' emphatically
jokes.explanation: Jerry's exasperation boiling over - simple but effective breakdown

jokes.text: Shouldn't there be some kind of reward for that? Otherwise you could die, if that bothers you.
jokes.setup: Jerry saves Jeannie from being hit by car
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion about death with 'if that bothers you' qualifier
jokes.explanation: Jerry's detached observation about death with perfect casual delivery

jokes.text: The shirt under your sweater. It sits in a drawer for three weeks... and when it finally comes out, it only sticks up out of your collar.
jokes.setup: Jeannie calls Jerry's collar shirt a waste
jokes.punchline: Detailed observation about shirt's limited visibility despite storage time
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific observation that sounds exactly like Jerry - perfect mirror character moment

jokes.text: Same initials. How do you like that? I like it.
jokes.setup: Jerry and Jeannie introduce themselves
jokes.punchline: Both being equally pleased by the initials coincidence
jokes.explanation: Sets up their similarity - both finding the same mundane thing noteworthy

jokes.text: He doesn't even know my name. That was an honest mistake. No, he's too weird. He'd fall or something. He'd ruin the whole ceremony.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Kramer as usher
jokes.punchline: Susan's specific fear that Kramer would literally fall during ceremony
jokes.explanation: Susan's reasonable fear of Kramer's physical comedy disrupting formal event

jokes.text: I've always smoked. I've never seen you smoke. Oh, yeah, well, big smoker.
jokes.setup: Susan catches George smoking for first time
jokes.punchline: George's obvious lie about being a 'big smoker'
jokes.explanation: George's transparent attempt to sabotage relationship - cringe but endearing

jokes.text: A bowl of Cheerios, not too much milk. Okay, two bowls of Cheerios. You too? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Jerry orders cereal at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Jeannie orders the exact same unusual thing
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of their uncanny similarity - both order breakfast cereal for dinner

jokes.text: Now I know what I've been looking for all these years. Myself. I've been waiting for me to come along. And now I've swept myself off my feet.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining his attraction to Jeannie
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's in love with himself, literally swept himself off his feet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant narcissistic revelation - Jerry's self-awareness about his self-love is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Will you stop it, man? You're freaking me out.
jokes.setup: Jerry's romantic soliloquy about dating himself
jokes.punchline: George's disturbed reaction to Jerry's narcissistic epiphany
jokes.explanation: George's reasonable disgust at Jerry's self-romance - good straight man reaction

jokes.text: Well, 'hey' is 'hello.' Same thing. The ad said the bank will pay $100 if you're not greeted with a 'hello.' You're taking that much too literally.
jokes.setup: Kramer demands $100 for not being greeted with 'hello'
jokes.punchline: Semantic argument over whether 'hey' equals 'hello'
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer literal interpretation of promotional offers - linguistic pedantry for profit

jokes.text: Now I'm gonna be stuck at the singles' table with all the losers? You can go with Kramer. No, no, no. Weddings are a great place to meet chicks. I have to be unfettered.
jokes.setup: George complains about being at singles table
jokes.punchline: George calling other singles 'losers' while needing to be 'unfettered' to meet women
jokes.explanation: George's complete lack of self-awareness - insulting other singles while being single himself

jokes.text: Ask her to sign a prenup. Because most women, when asked to sign a prenup... are so offended, they back out of the marriage.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggests prenup as escape strategy
jokes.punchline: Using prenup specifically to sabotage relationship rather than protect assets
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of prenup purpose - using legal protection as relationship destroyer

jokes.text: What's so funny? You don't have any money. I make more money than you do. Yeah, give me the papers, I'll sign them.
jokes.setup: George nervously asks Susan to sign prenup
jokes.punchline: Susan's amusement at protecting George's non-existent wealth, eager to sign
jokes.explanation: Perfect backfire - Susan's practical response ruins George's escape plan

jokes.text: 'Hey' is the same as 'hello.' What do you think? Yeah, I think it's the same thing. Oh, big surprise.
jokes.setup: Kramer asks Jeannie about hey vs hello
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic 'big surprise' at their agreement
jokes.explanation: Jerry's mounting frustration with dating his clone - their inevitable agreement annoys him

jokes.text: Oh, these are so cheap. Don't forget tomorrow. We're shopping for rings... so don't make any plans. And this time we're not skimping.
jokes.setup: Susan complains about cheap wedding invitations
jokes.punchline: Insisting on expensive rings after choosing toxic cheap invitations
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony - Susan's desire for quality now, after the fatal cheap choice was made

jokes.text: I don't like her! I don't like her. I never liked her from the get-go. Everything she thinks, you think. Everything you think, she thinks. No, I can't take it.
jokes.setup: Jerry asks what's wrong with Jeannie
jokes.punchline: Elaine's complete breakdown over Jerry dating his female clone
jokes.explanation: Elaine's visceral reaction to Jerry's narcissistic relationship - great emotional escalation

jokes.text: If you can't take her, maybe you can't take me. So that's how it's gonna be? That's how it's gonna be. Oh, God help us!
jokes.setup: Elaine rejects Jerry's girlfriend who's exactly like him
jokes.punchline: Jerry's logical conclusion threatening their friendship, Elaine's dramatic despair
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical trap - Jerry forces Elaine to confront her feelings about him through proxy

jokes.text: Yeah, but that's not 'hello.' That's a tough one. Let me bring some other people in on this.
jokes.setup: Bank manager mediating hey vs hello dispute
jokes.punchline: Treating semantic argument as serious banking matter requiring committee
jokes.explanation: Bureaucratic escalation of absurd problem - treating linguistics as corporate crisis

jokes.text: Well, we've discussed this. Here's the feeling. You got a greeting. It starts with an H. How's 20 bucks sound?
jokes.setup: Bank committee deliberates greeting policy
jokes.punchline: Compromise solution: $20 for H-greeting instead of full $100
jokes.explanation: Brilliant corporate compromise - splitting difference on semantic technicality with corporate logic

jokes.text: September 21st, first day of autumn. Leaves changing, beautiful colours. All that crap.
jokes.setup: Jerry announces engagement with romantic imagery
jokes.punchline: Dismissing romantic details as 'all that crap'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry deflation of romantic sentiment - builds it up just to dismiss it

jokes.text: Hey, what's the deal with decaf? How do they get the caffeine out of there? And then where does it go?
jokes.setup: Jeannie ordering coffee
jokes.punchline: Classic Jerry-style 'what's the deal' observational bit from his clone
jokes.explanation: Perfect mimicry of Jerry's comedy style - uncanny valley of relationship

jokes.text: If it's a combination of breakfast and lunch... how come there's no 'lupper,' or no 'linner'?
jokes.setup: Jeannie commenting on brunch concept
jokes.punchline: Creating 'lupper' and 'linner' as logical extensions
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that sounds exactly like Jerry - creates new portmanteau words logically

jokes.text: I can't be with someone like me. I hate myself! I need to be with someone the complete opposite of me.
jokes.setup: Jerry realizes problem with dating his clone
jokes.punchline: Brutal self-awareness: he hates himself, needs his opposite
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-loathing revelation - Jerry's narcissism crashes into self-awareness

jokes.text: She expired. Are you sure? Yes, of course. So... she's dead? Yes.
jokes.setup: Doctor announces Susan's death
jokes.punchline: George's need for multiple confirmations of death with casual delivery
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - George's inability to process shocking news with appropriate gravity

jokes.text: We found traces... of a certain toxic adhesive... commonly found in very low-priced envelopes. Well, she was sending out our wedding invitations. That's probably what did it.
jokes.setup: Doctor explains cause of death
jokes.punchline: George's matter-of-fact acceptance that cheap invitations killed his fiancée
jokes.explanation: Masterpiece of dark comedy - George's penny-pinching literally kills Susan, delivered with casual acceptance

jokes.text: We were expecting about 200 people.
jokes.setup: Doctor says toxic glue from wedding invitations killed Susan
jokes.punchline: George's casual detail about guest count in response to death news
jokes.explanation: Incredible dark comedy - George treats death like wedding planning detail, perfect emotional detachment

jokes.text: Poor Lilly. Susan. Susan.
jokes.setup: Kramer expressing sympathy for Susan's death
jokes.punchline: Still gets her name wrong even at death announcement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer moment - even death can't help him remember her actual name

jokes.text: We had a pact! Well, let's get some coffee.
jokes.setup: Jerry protests George not being married as promised
jokes.punchline: George's casual transition to coffee after fiancée's death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant transition - George treating death like minor scheduling conflict, moving to mundane activity

jokes.text: I'm the short, funny, quirky bald man you met a little while ago. Yeah, I was just calling because I wanted you to know... that I'm not engaged anymore. Well, she died.
jokes.setup: George calling Marisa Tomei after Susan's death
jokes.punchline: Casual announcement of death as dating availability update
jokes.explanation: Stunning dark comedy finale - George using death as pickup line, treating tragedy as opportunity

jokes.text: Well, we were expecting about 200 people. Yeah. Anyway, I've got the funeral tomorrow... but my weekend is pretty wide open...
jokes.setup: George explaining Susan's death to potential date
jokes.punchline: Scheduling funeral between weekend dating plans
jokes.explanation: Absolute peak dark comedy - George treats funeral as minor scheduling obstacle to dating, perfect character consistency
